ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-person Subway combo meal. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKR (Sandwiches) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a bundled two-person Subway meal combo.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a commercial fast-food sandwich combo meal for two persons.

main_ingredients: choice of protein (chicken, tuna, beef, or plant-based)
salad vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, capsicum, olives)

serving.description: 1 meal for 2 persons (2 footlong or 4 six-inch sub sandwiches with sides and drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: A Classic Meal for Two at Subway Singapore typically comprises two full submarine sandwiches (each approximately 300 g), two side items (approximately 50 g each), and two beverages (approximately 250 ml each), totalling approximately 900 g of solid food per meal occasion.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Footlong Sandwich (average of standard fillings) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Subway Singapore nutritional panel estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Subway Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a Subway Classic Meal for Two as a composite product. Per-100g values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for submarine sandwich components (bread, mixed protein fillings, salad vegetables, condiments) weighted to reflect a typical Subway six-inch sandwich composition. The meal bundle includes beverages and sides; the per-100g figures represent the solid sandwich component only, consistent with standard food-database practice for composite sandwich entries. Values are cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry #172052 (Submarine sandwich, with tuna) and #172053 (Submarine sandwich, with roast beef) as analogous references.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based sandwiches — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white submarine bread and mixed-filling sandwiches
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Classic Meal for Two composite. GI of 53 is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white submarine-style bread (GI approximately 53–59, Atkinson et al. 2008) and mixed-filling sandwiches. The protein and fat content of the filling moderates the overall GI relative to plain bread. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100: serving carbohydrate estimated at 81 g for two sandwiches (900 g × 0.285 × 0.32 solid fraction adjustment); GL = 53 × 81 / 100 ≈ 43.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; estimated ~1,400–1,800 mg per full two-person meal)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium load from bread, processed meats, cheese, and condiments, which can significantly elevate blood pressure; requesting low-sodium fillings and avoiding added sauces can partially mitigate risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~53) with high total carbohydrate load per meal occasion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of the high total carbohydrate load per meal (estimated 80–90 g per person) despite the moderate GI; choosing wholegrain bread, lean protein fillings, and avoiding sugary beverages and cookies can substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and saturated fat from processed meats, cheese, and condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit this meal due to its combined sodium and saturated fat burden; selecting grilled lean protein, omitting cheese, and choosing water over sugary drinks are recommended modifications to reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Classic Meal for Two is a bundled fast-food meal available at Subway restaurants in Singapore, priced at approximately SGD 25.80–30.30. The meal typically comprises two submarine sandwiches (each a footlong or two six-inch subs), two side items such as chips or cookies, and two beverages. Each sandwich is built on a soft white or wholegrain submarine roll and customised with a choice of protein (such as chicken teriyaki, tuna, roast beef, or a plant-based option), fresh salad vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, capsicum, olives), optional cheese, and a selection of sauces. The bread is soft and slightly chewy, with a mild yeasty flavour; fillings provide a contrast of textures from crisp vegetables and tender protein. Nutritionally, the meal is moderately energy-dense at approximately 218 kcal per 100 g of sandwich, with a meaningful protein contribution (~11 g/100 g) and significant sodium (~520 mg/100 g) from bread, processed meats, and condiments. The glycemic index of the sandwich component is estimated at 53 (low-moderate), though the total carbohydrate load per person is high. Clinically, the meal is of concern for hypertension due to sodium, and requires carbohydrate management for Type 2 Diabetes. It is widely available across Singapore via field survey and in-store, and is popular as a shared lunch or dinner option for two diners seeking a customisable Western-style fast-food meal.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a Subway Classic Meal for Two as a composite product. Per-100g nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy submarine sandwich entries (analogous references #172052 and #172053) weighted to reflect typical Subway Singapore filling compositions. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white submarine bread. Confidence is medium because the estimation is grounded in approved sources but is not an exact match for this specific bundled product.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central submarine sandwich reference entries used for this estimation. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA reference entries. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA reference entries. 4. Per-100g values represent the solid sandwich component only; the beverage and side-item components of the meal bundle are excluded from the nutrient profile per standard food-database practice. 5. The meal is sold under the promotional name 'Classic Meal for 2 (U.P. $30.30)' on field survey data; the canonical food name has been normalised to 'Subway Classic Meal for Two' for database purposes. 6. Halal status is set to true as Subway Singapore operates under a halal-certified supply chain for its Singapore outlets. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database white submarine bread entries; no direct GI measurement for this composite product exists in the approved literature. 8. GL per serving (~43) is calculated for the full two-person solid sandwich component (~900 g, ~28.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g); this is a high GL meal occasion when shared between two persons (~21.5 GL per person).
